cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
cel: noun. A piece of celluloid on which has been drawn a frame of an animated film.
cere: noun. A fleshy, waxy area at the base of the upper beak in certain birds.
cheer: noun. A cheerful attitude; happiness; a good, happy, or positive mood.
chef: noun. The presiding cook in the kitchen of a large household.
churl: noun. A free peasant (as opposed to a serf) of the lowest rank, below an earl and a thane; a freeman; also, a person without royal or noble
clef: noun. A symbol found on a musical staff that indicates the pitches represented by the lines and the spaces on the staff
clue: noun. A strand of yarn etc. as used to guide one through a labyrinth; something which points the way, a guide.
creel: noun. A woven basket, especially a wicker basket.
cruel: adjective. Intentionally causing or reveling in pain and suffering; merciless, heartless.
cue: noun. The name of the Latin script letter Q / q.
cur: noun. A contemptible or inferior dog.
cure: noun. A method, device or medication that restores good health.
curl: noun. A curving piece or lock of hair; a ringlet.
ecu: noun. The European Currency Unit (symbol ₠), a currency used in the European Community before the euro.
eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
elf: noun. A supernatural being or spirit associated with illness, mischief, and harmful or dangerous magical influence; in later Norse sources,
euchre: noun. A trump card game played by four players in two partnerships with a reduced deck of 24 cards.
fee: noun. An amount charged for a privilege.
feel: verb. To use or experience the sense of touch.
feh: interjection. An expression of disgust or contempt.
fere: noun. A companion, comrade or friend.
ferule: noun. A ruler - shaped instrument, generally used to slap naughty children on the hand.
feu: noun. Land held in feudal tenure.
flee: verb. To run away; to escape.
fleech: verb. To wheedle; coax; cajole; induce with fair words; flatter.
fleer: verb. To make a wry face in contempt, or to grin in scorn
flue: noun. A pipe or duct that carries gaseous combustion products away from the point of combustion (such as a furnace).
fuel: noun. Substance consumed to provide energy through combustion, or through chemical or nuclear reaction.
fueler: noun. One who, or that which, supplies fuel.
fur: noun. The hairy coat of various mammal species, especially when fine, soft and thick.
furl: verb. To lower, roll up and secure (something, such as a sail or flag)
heel: noun. The rear part of the foot, where it joins the leg.
her: determiner. Belonging to her (belonging to that female person or animal, or in poetic or old-fashioned language that ship, city, season,
here: adverb. In, on, or at this place (a place perceived to be close to the speaker); compare there.
hue: noun. A color, or shade of color; tint; dye.
lech: noun. A strong, lecherous desire or craving.
lecher: noun. A lecherous person: someone given to excessive sexual activity or debauchery.
lee: noun. A protected cove or harbor, out of the wind.
leech: noun. A typically aquatic blood - sucking annelid of the subclass Hirudinea, especially Hirudo medicinalis.
leer: verb. To look sideways or obliquely; now especially with sexual desire or malicious intent.
lehr: noun. A long oven or kiln, often having a moving belt, used for annealing glass.
leu: noun. The unit of currency of Romania, equal to one hundred bani.
luce: noun. The pike, Esox lucius, when fully grown.
lucre: noun. Money, riches, or wealth, especially when seen as having a corrupting effect or causing greed, or obtained in an underhanded manner.
lurch: noun. A sudden or unsteady movement.
lure: noun. Something that tempts or attracts, especially one with a promise of reward or pleasure.
ree: noun. Alternative form of rei (“Portuguese real”).
reef: noun. A chain or range of rocks, sand, or coral lying at or near the surface of the water.
reel: noun. A shaky or unsteady gait.
refel: verb. To refute, disprove (an argument); to confute (someone).
ruche: noun. A strip of fabric which has been fluted or pleated.
ulcer: noun. An open sore of the skin, eyes or mucous membrane, often caused by an initial abrasion and generally maintained by an inflammation
celure: noun. A canopy, especially one over a bed or altar.
chee: noun. Synonym of mace (“old Chinese unit of weight”).
churel: noun. In Hindu folklore, the ghost of a woman who died during childbirth, pregnancy; or a witch.
crl: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Northern East Cree.
cru: noun. A vineyard or group of vineyards in France, especially one having a high reputation.
cul: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Culina.
feer: adjective. Alternative form of fear (“able, capable”).
heer: noun. A yarn measure of six hundred yards, or 1/24 of a spindle.
huer: noun. Someone who cries out or gives an alarm, especially A balker or conder; one who watches shoals of fish so that they can be caught.
lue: verb. To sift using a sieve, particularly in mining tin or silver.
lur: noun. A long natural blowing horn without finger holes, played by embouchure.
recule: verb. To recoil or retreat; to draw back.
ule: noun. A Mexican and Central American tree (Castilla elastica), related to the breadfruit tree, whose milky juice contains caoutchouc.
urf: noun. The customs of an Arab society, used in jurisprudence when the Qur'an and Sunnah do not mandate a ruling.
